Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The intended order is R A E P N C

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have a DECLARE model in Python dictionary with anomalies init R existence C responded existence E with activities A precedence C with activities R noncoexistence E with activities C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The noncoexistence rule disallows presence of both E and C yet business logic requires E before C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means valid traces cannot include both evaluation and closing events which contradicts the intended process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The model does not mention P and N so there may be incomplete modeling of the full process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: It might be due to misinterpretation of process flow requirements

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: It could also reflect inconsistent incremental changes in policies where some constraints were updated but others were not

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: There might be technical issues such as incomplete data transformation from logs to the DECLARE model

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Pressure for speed in claims processing might lead to skipping evaluation steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims closed without any evaluation event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims that have both E and C events in the same trace

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to check whether every evaluation event has a preceding assignment event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to check whether every closing event has a preceding receive event
